sourceMovie.title: Hard Boiled
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hard Boiled works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Contraband
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Contraband (2012) operates in the same action as Hard Boiled, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Infernal Affairs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Infernal Affairs (2002) belongs to the same conversation as Hard Boiled — both anchor their action in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Killer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hard Boiled's action stayed with you, The Killer (1989) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Police Story 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Hard Boiled excels in action, Police Story 2 (1988) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Vanishing Point
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Vanishing Point (1971) is the natural next step after Hard Boiled: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Year of the Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Hard Boiled who respond to its action will find Year of the Dragon (1985) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Magnum Force
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Hard Boiled fans keep landing on Magnum Force (1973) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Enforcer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Enforcer draws from the same action well as Hard Boiled, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1976 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Master Z: Ip Man Legacy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Master Z: Ip Man Legacy (2018) captures the action appeal of Hard Boiled without simply imitating it. The crime streak running through both films is what puts Master Z: Ip Man Legacy at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: New Jack City
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of Hard Boiled — that dark edge, the crime — translates cleanly into New Jack City (1991). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hard Boiled?
faq.answer: Try Contraband, Infernal Affairs, The Killer—each shares DNA with Hard Boiled while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hard Boiled?
faq.answer: Hard Boiled (1992) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hard Boiled worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
